goal: Major Goal: Finite-to-Type-II Static-Patch Observer Algebra
claim_boundary: finite-to-operator-algebra theorem candidate only; no continuum de Sitter, dS/CFT, or literal ER=EPR theorem is claimed

major_claims.claim: The raw consecutive fuzzy-sphere cutoffs do not form a unital trace-preserving full-matrix inductive system.
A cofinal factorial cutoff subsequence gives unital trace-preserving matrix inclusions.
The diagonal screen inclusions preserve normalized trace data by equal atom splitting.
The quantum inductive limit is a UHF algebra whose tracial GNS closure is a hyperfinite Type II_1 candidate.
The dephased control has the same screen shadows but an abelian von Neumann limit.
A noncommutative matrix-unit commutator witness persists under trace-preserving inclusions.
The strong-continuity bridge diagnostic from Goal 31 is preserved.
Convergence of modular/KMS semigroups still requires inclusion-covariant static-patch generators.

frontier: derive inclusion-covariant static-patch generators from fuzzy-sphere or continuum modular dynamics
replace the factorial subsequence with a canonical static-patch inclusion if one exists
compare the finite hyperfinite Type II_1 candidate to CLPW/Witten static-patch observer algebras
